_publications._publicationTitle: Multidimensional Immunotyping of Human NF1-Associated Peripheral Nerve Sheath Tumors Uncovers Tumor-Associated Macrophages as Key Drivers of Immune Evasion in the Tumor Microenvironment.

_contextSnippet: CD19+ B cells had an overall low density across PN, ANNUBP, and MPNST tumors. Their infiltration was more varied and significantly higher in MPNST samples compared to PN and ANNUBP samples
details: Successfully detected B cells with low overall density across tumor types; highest median density in MPNST (0.85 cells/mm²) compared to PN (0.19 cells/mm²) and ANNUBP (0.02 cells/mm²)
